attributes.action: Cap donations to political parties from individuals and businesses
attributes.additional_details: History proves money buys influence. Bernie Ecclestone donated £1m to Labour and received a tobacco advertising exemption. The “Cash for Honours” scandal saw large donors nominated for peerages. Nearly 1 in 5 elite Conservative donors received honours. In 2025, one individual gave £9m to Reform UK. The Bribery Act 2010 makes it illegal to pay for favourable treatment — yet political donations escape this entirely. We need a legal donation cap, tighter loopholes, and stronger penalties now.
attributes.background: UK political parties face no legal limit on donations, allowing wealthy individuals and corporations to buy political influence. This undermines democracy, contradicts anti-bribery law, and means policy decisions can be shaped by the richest donors rather than the needs of ordinary citizens.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign this petition calling for similar action:

'Donations to a political party capped at £500,000 per individual or organisation' https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/760491
